sourceMovie.title: Constantine
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Constantine works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Constantine clicked for you, Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Nimona
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Constantine clicked for you, Nimona (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Infinity War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Constantine clicked for you, Avengers: Infinity War (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Howl's Moving Castle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Constantine clicked for you, Howl's Moving Castle (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Constantine clicked for you, Beetlejuice (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Constantine clicked for you, Insidious (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Constantine?
faq.answer: Try Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness, Nimona, Avengers: Infinity War—each shares DNA with Constantine while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Constantine?
faq.answer: Constantine (2005) is best described as Fantasy, Action, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Constantine part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Constantine Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
